problem.title: Print Linked List in Reversed Order
problem.description: Print a linked list in reversed order using recursion or iterative approach.

example.explanation: Traverse to end using recursion (call stack), print on way back. Or reverse then print.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Recursive [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Recurse to the end of the list before printing: each call first processes the rest of the list, then prints the current node's value on the way back up. This naturally prints nodes in reverse without any explicit reversal or extra data structure.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def printLinkedListInReverse(head: Optional[object]) -> None:
    if not head:
        return
    printLinkedListInReverse(head.next)
    print(head.val)

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Stack-Based Iterative [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Traverse the linked list from head to tail, pushing each node's value onto a stack. Then pop all values from the stack and collect/print them — since a stack is LIFO, this reverses the order. O(n) time O(n) space for the stack.
